insight: Curaçao's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup shows a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they experienced a slight decline in momentum, with a mean change of -0.050 per minute across the two breaks, indicating that the breaks may have slightly hindered their progress. When Curaçao was pressing before a hydration break, they were able to maintain momentum only half of the time, with the same average change of -0.050 per minute, suggesting that their aggressive play did not necessarily give them an advantage during the breaks. Since there were no instances where Curaçao was not pressing before a break, it's difficult to draw conclusions about how they perform in those situations, but overall, the breaks seem to have had a neutral to slightly negative effect on their momentum.
